title: RunScore
description: One score written against a run by an external grader. Returned by POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/scores (201) and listed by GET /v1/runs/{run_id}/scores. No uniqueness on (run_id, name, evaluator) — re-grading is a write, not an update; the historical sequence is preserved by `created_at`.

id.description: Server-assigned row id. Populated on response; absent on request.

run_id.description: The run this score is against. Must reference an existing row in `runs`.

name.description: Score name (e.g. 'helpfulness', 'factuality'). Charset-restricted because it flows into log lines and aggregate group-by queries.

value.description: Score value. Unconstrained range because rubrics differ (0..1 normalized, 1..5 Likert, raw token counts, latency budgets, etc.). Consumers normalize as they need.

evaluator.description: Who or what produced the score (e.g. 'gpt-4o-judge', 'human:reviewer-3', 'rubric/v2'). Charset-restricted for the same reasons as `name`, but allows ':' and '/' for namespacing.

comment.description: Free-form grader rationale. Defaults to null.

metadata.description: Opaque grader-supplied metadata (rubric version, model temperature, dataset slice, etc.).

created_at.description: Server-assigned creation timestamp. Populated on response.
